channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Can’t see the markings, 2nd May
item.description:
We can’t see the markings outside if the Mcdonald kingditch lane towards to big roundabout
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsditch Lane, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: Sunken Infill In Trench, 2nd May
item.description:
The tarmac in a trench dug right across the road has sunk, leaving a very sharp edged obstacle that smashes through car suspension when driven over. I notice this issue has been reported several times already and don’t understand how a hazard created by shoddy roadworks can be left un-repaired for months.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous deterioration of road surface, 1st May
item.description:
The road surface at the junction of Old Bath Road and Leckhampton Road heading down Old Bath Road has a significant number of very large potholes. These are situated where cars turn into Old Bath Road, which creates a safety risk while turning onto a cratered and uneven road. This issue has been present for a long time.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 381, Old Bath Road, Leckhampton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Verge not mowed by council contractor, 1st May
item.description:
Verge bordering 1 Barton Close on Bafford Approach and Barton Close was not mowed by the council contractors today and requires maintenance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11, Bafford Approach, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
